task_completion.description: The dive log application (Subsurface) is opened and displays the dive list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface GUI is visible on the screen showing a list of dives.

task_completion.description: Dives are sorted, filtered, or multi-selected based on a maximum depth of 18.0m or greater.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive list is sorted by the 'Depth' column, a filter for depth >= 18.0m is applied, or multiple dives with depth >= 18.0m are highlighted/selected.

task_completion.description: The tag 'Deep' is added to the dives with a depth of 18.0m or greater.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'Deep' is visible in the 'Tags' input field or tag display area for the selected deep dives.

task_completion.description: Dives with a depth of less than 18.0m do not have the 'Deep' tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When a shallow dive (< 18.0m) is visible or selected, the 'Deep' tag is NOT present in its tags list.

task_completion.description: The updated dive log is saved to /home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog is visible with the path '/home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf', or the main application window title bar updates to show 'dives.ssrf' in the Documents folder.

integrity.description: The agent used the application's GUI to tag the dives.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface application interface (selecting dives, typing in the tags box) rather than editing an XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal scripts to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run Python, bash, or sed/awk commands that automatically modify the .ssrf file.

integrity.description: The tag was genuinely applied within the application's designated tag field.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Deep' tag is typed into the actual Subsurface 'Tags' field in the Information panel, not just written in notes or a separate document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target logbook contains 29 real dives. Exactly 17 dives have a max depth >= 18.0m and must be tagged 'Deep' (dive numbers: 86, 87, 89, 90, 93, 94, 95, 96, 223, 224, 226, 227, 229, 231, 232, 233, 333). The other 12 dives (dive numbers: 2, 3, 4, 5, 85, 88, 91, 92, 225, 228, 230, 348) are shallower and must NOT be tagged 'Deep'. The final file must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf'.
